mnemonics: S.P.I.R.A.L. (Software, Planning, Incremental, Risk, Analysis, Linear) for remembering the concurrent model's iterative phases.
keyPoints: Development activities (modeling, construction, deployment) happen simultaneously in different iterations.
It allows for more flexibility and faster delivery of increments compared to a linear sequential model.
It is particularly effective for projects where requirements are not fully understood upfront and may evolve.
commonMistakes: Confusing it with the Waterfall model, which is strictly linear and sequential with no overlap.
Poorly managing communication and integration between concurrent activities, leading to chaos and defects.
Assuming it requires less planning; it actually requires robust planning to manage the concurrent workflows effectively.
